name: My OSINT Training Tools
description: Curated, categorized web tool launcher maintained by My OSINT Training (Griffin Glynn).
bestFor: Quickly launching vetted OSINT web tools by category
trustNote: Maintained by Griffin Glynn (@hatless1der), well-known OSINT trainer; curator of this board.
notes: A meta tool-collection rather than a single tool. Strong starting point for people/username/social lookups. Featured (starred) on the board.

name: Bellingcat's Online Investigation Toolkit
description: Bellingcat's continuously maintained GitBook of investigation tools across many categories.
bestFor: Authoritative, current list of investigation tools by category
trustNote: Maintained by Bellingcat, a leading open-source investigation organization.
notes: Meta-collection. Excellent for geolocation, social media, maps, and verification tools relevant to locating people. Featured (starred) on the board.

name: cyb_detective OSINT stuff tool collection
description: Large categorized index of 1000+ OSINT tools and services by cipher387 (cyb_detective).
bestFor: Browsing an enormous categorized index of niche OSINT tools
trustNote: Maintained by cipher387/cyb_detective, prolific OSINT tool curator.
notes: Meta-collection on GitHub Pages; has a companion GitHub repo. Deep coverage of username, email, phone, and image search tools. Featured (starred) on the board.

name: IntelTechniques OSINT Search Tool
description: Michael Bazzell's classic suite of online search forms for usernames, emails, phones, names, and more.
bestFor: Structured query-building across many platforms from one page
trustNote: Created by Michael Bazzell (IntelTechniques), foundational figure in OSINT.
notes: Browser-side search-form generator (no data is sent to the site). Directly useful for pivoting on names, usernames, emails, and phones. Featured (starred) on the board.

name: AaronCTI's Online Resources
description: Public Google Sheet of OSINT/CTI online resources curated by AaronCTI.
bestFor: Browsing a community-maintained spreadsheet of resources
trustNote: Community-maintained Google Sheet; quality varies by row.
notes: Mixed OSINT and threat-intel resources. Situational for missing-persons work.

name: Aware Online OSINT Tools
description: Aware Online academy's set of free OSINT tools (username, social media, image, etc.).
bestFor: Free single-purpose lookup tools by an OSINT training academy
trustNote: Aware Online is an established OSINT training provider.
notes: Includes username checkers and social media search helpers useful for locating people.

name: CyberSecStu - OSINT for Finding People
description: Public Google Sheet focused specifically on tools for finding people (CyberSecStu).
bestFor: People-finding-specific tool list in spreadsheet form
trustNote: Community spreadsheet; explicitly themed around finding people.
notes: Directly themed for people-finding; high relevance to missing-persons workflows.

name: Google Hacking Database (GHDB)
description: Exploit-DB's searchable database of Google dorks / advanced search queries.
bestFor: Finding ready-made advanced Google dorks for targeted searches
trustNote: Maintained by Offensive Security / Exploit-DB.
notes: Dorks can surface exposed documents, profiles, and directories mentioning a person; medium relevance, more infra/exposure focused.

name: FBI-tools by Daniel Durnea
description: GitHub awesome-list of OSINT tools spanning many investigation categories.
bestFor: Browsing a broad GitHub-hosted awesome-list of OSINT tools
trustNote: Community GitHub awesome-list.
notes: Aggregates links across many categories; quality varies. Useful as a frontier index.

name: GIJN Online Research Tools
description: Global Investigative Journalism Network's curated research tools page.
bestFor: Journalism-grade vetted research and verification tools
trustNote: GIJN is a respected global journalism organization.
notes: Strong for verification, records, and people-tracing resources used by investigative journalists.

name: Google Advanced Search Operators (guide)
description: Reference document listing Google advanced search operators.
bestFor: Reference for building precise Google queries
trustNote: Community reference document.
notes: Reference material, not a tool, but supports targeted name/location searches.

name: i-intelligence OSINT Handbook (2025)
description: The widely-used i-intelligence OSINT Handbook of tools and resources.
bestFor: Comprehensive PDF/web compendium of OSINT tools and methods
trustNote: Long-standing, well-regarded OSINT handbook (i-intelligence).
notes: Email registration may be required to download the handbook PDF. Broad reference covering people, social, and geolocation resources.

name: Intelligence X Tools
description: Collection of free utilities by Intelligence X (search engine for leaks, darkweb, archives).
bestFor: Free utility tools plus access to IntelX's leak/archive search
trustNote: Intelligence X is an established search engine for leaked/archived data.
notes: Free tools include various lookups; full leak/archive search and API are gated/freemium. Useful for finding records and breach references tied to a person.

name: IVMachiavelli OSINT Team Links
description: GitHub-hosted curated list of OSINT links/tools.
bestFor: Browsing a community OSINT link directory
trustNote: Community GitHub list.
notes: Aggregator list; verify freshness of individual links.

name: jivoi/awesome-osint
description: One of the most popular GitHub awesome-osint curated lists.
bestFor: Comprehensive, well-maintained GitHub awesome-list of OSINT tools
trustNote: Highly-starred, long-standing community awesome-list.
notes: Excellent frontier source: covers people-search, username, email, phone, social, image categories in a parseable README.

name: Journalist's Toolbox - Investigative
description: SPJ-associated curated set of investigative research tools for journalists.
bestFor: Journalist-oriented investigative and people-tracing tools
trustNote: Long-running journalism resource (Mike Reilley).
notes: Good for public-records and people-finding tools, US-centric in parts.

name: Malfrat's OSINT Map
description: Interactive visual mind-map of OSINT tools organized by investigation type.
bestFor: Visually navigating tool categories from a starting selector
trustNote: Community-maintained interactive OSINT mind-map.
notes: Branches by selector (username, email, phone, image, etc.) making it easy to find the right tool for a given lead.

name: MetaOSINT (IntelScott)
description: Searchable, sortable table of top OSINT tools and resources.
bestFor: Filtering a ranked table of tools by category and type
trustNote: Maintained by IntelScott; data-driven ranking of tools.
notes: One of the best structured, filterable indexes; strong for systematically picking people/username/phone tools.

name: Offensive Security Cheatsheet (haax.fr)
description: Pentest/offensive-security cheatsheet that includes OSINT reconnaissance sections.
bestFor: Command and technique references for recon and OSINT
trustNote: Community offensive-security cheatsheet.
notes: Mostly infra/pentest focus; OSINT recon section is the relevant slice. Low relevance to missing persons.

name: OfficerCia Non-Typical OSINT Guide
description: GitHub guide of less-common OSINT techniques and tools, incl. crypto and privacy.
bestFor: Discovering non-obvious OSINT techniques and niche tools
trustNote: Well-known community guide by OffcierCia.
notes: Covers unconventional pivots; some crypto/financial focus. Situational.

name: Toddington Online Research Cheat Sheets
description: Toddington International's free cheat sheets for online research.
bestFor: Quick-reference cheat sheets for platform-specific searching
trustNote: Toddington is an established online-research training firm.
notes: Reference material for searching specific platforms; supports people lookups.

name: OSINT STASH
description: Curated, categorized directory of OSINT tools (osint.best).
bestFor: Browsing a clean modern directory of OSINT tools by category
trustNote: Community-curated tool directory.
notes: Well-organized index spanning people, username, email, image, and social categories.

name: OSINT Combine Tools
description: OSINT Combine's free tools (NexusXplore vendor) incl. social and language utilities.
bestFor: Free single-purpose tools from a commercial OSINT vendor
trustNote: OSINT Combine is a reputable commercial OSINT training/tooling company.
notes: Includes free utilities (e.g., username/social, language tools); flagship NexusXplore platform is paid.

name: OSINT Encyclopedia (optiv / cham423)
description: Optiv-published GitHub encyclopedia of OSINT resources and techniques.
bestFor: Reference encyclopedia of OSINT methods and tools
trustNote: Published by Optiv (security firm) on GitHub.
notes: Reference-style repo; parseable on GitHub. Broad coverage.

name: OSINT Essentials
description: Verification-focused tool and resource site for journalists/researchers.
bestFor: Content verification, image/video, and geolocation tools
trustNote: Long-running verification resource (First Draft lineage).
notes: Strong for verifying and geolocating images/video that may show a missing person or location.

name: OSINT Framework
description: The classic tree-style web index of OSINT tools organized by data type.
bestFor: Navigating tools by selector type from a single tree
trustNote: Iconic, widely-referenced OSINT tool tree (Justin Nordine).
notes: Foundational index; branches include Username, Email, Phone, People Search, Social Networks. Some links may be stale.

name: OSINT Investigation Assistant (lambda.black)
description: Single-page assistant linking OSINT tools/queries for investigations.
bestFor: Quick links and query helpers on one page
trustNote: Personal/community page.
notes: Lightweight assistant page; situational utility.

name: OH SHINT! OSINT Web Resources
description: GitBook of OSINT web resources and methodology.
bestFor: Methodology-rich walkthrough of web resources by topic
trustNote: Community GitBook (OH SHINT!).
notes: Combines tools with how-to guidance; useful for technique discovery.

name: OSINT Techniques Tools
description: Michael James / OSINT Techniques curated tools page.
bestFor: A practitioner-curated categorized tools page
trustNote: OSINT Techniques is a well-regarded practitioner site/book author.
notes: Clean categorized index spanning people, username, email, phone, and image tools.

name: Osint.link
description: Directory-style portal of OSINT links organized by category.
bestFor: Browsing a large categorized portal of OSINT links
trustNote: Community link portal.
notes: Broad portal; verify link freshness.

name: OSINT Dojo Resources
description: OSINT Dojo's learning-oriented resources and tool references.
bestFor: Learning OSINT methodology with associated tools
trustNote: OSINT Dojo is a recognized training community.
notes: Training-oriented; includes a useful resources/tools index and flowcharts.

name: OSINTgeek Tools
description: OSINTgeek's curated set of tools and dashboards.
bestFor: Curated tools and custom search dashboards
trustNote: Community OSINT resource (osintgeek.de).
notes: Some tools/dashboards may require a free account. EU-aware coverage.

name: Ph055a/OSINT_Collection
description: GitHub maintained list of OSINT resources across categories.
bestFor: Browsing a categorized GitHub OSINT resource list
trustNote: Community GitHub list.
notes: Parseable README; aggregator. Good frontier source.

name: Plessas Digital Knowledge Base
description: Plessas Experts Network's public knowledge base of search techniques and tools.
bestFor: Search technique references and tool pointers
trustNote: Plessas is a known search/research training provider.
notes: Notion-hosted knowledge base of techniques; supports advanced people/record searches.

name: r3mlab datajournalism-resources
description: GitHub collection of data-journalism and OSINT research resources.
bestFor: Data-journalism oriented research tools and datasets
trustNote: Community GitHub list (r3mlab).
notes: More data/dataset focused; lower direct relevance to person-finding.

name: OSINT for Countries (wddadk)
description: GitHub repo mapping OSINT resources by country.
bestFor: Finding country-specific records and registries
trustNote: Community GitHub list organized by country.
notes: Valuable when a lead is tied to a specific country; points to local registries and people-search resources.

name: Reuser's Repertorium
description: Arno Reuser's long-standing repertorium of OSINT sources and tools.
bestFor: Deep reference list from a veteran OSINT practitioner
trustNote: Arno Reuser is a pioneering OSINT practitioner.
notes: Served over plain HTTP; classic deep reference. Coverage includes registries, maps, and people sources.

name: sinwindie/OSINT
description: GitHub OSINT repo with workflows and platform-specific resources by Sinwindie.
bestFor: Platform-specific (social media) investigation workflows
trustNote: Sinwindie is a respected community contributor on social-media OSINT.
notes: Organized by social platform with workflow guidance; strong for tracing people across social media.

name: The Cyber Post OSINT Tools & Resources
description: Blog-hosted aggregated list of OSINT tools and resources.
bestFor: A broad aggregated tools listing
trustNote: Community blog aggregation.
notes: Aggregator on a security blog; verify currency.

name: The OSINT Rack (Mario Santella)
description: Personal curated 'rack' of OSINT tools by Mario Santella.
bestFor: A practitioner's hand-picked tool shortlist
trustNote: Personal site of practitioner Mario Santella.
notes: Curated shortlist; useful as a vetted starting set.

name: The OSINTion Wiki
description: Wiki of OSINT tools, techniques, and training by The OSINTion (Joe Gray).
bestFor: Wiki-style reference on tools and techniques
trustNote: The OSINTion (Joe Gray) is a recognized OSINT trainer/author.
notes: Structured wiki; combines tooling with methodology.

name: Toddington Free OSINT Resources
description: Toddington International's large curated list of free OSINT search/research tools.
bestFor: A vetted, professionally-maintained free tools list
trustNote: Toddington is an established online-research training firm.
notes: Comprehensive professional list spanning people search, public records, social, and image tools.

name: UK-OSINT
description: UK-focused OSINT resource portal (people, records, registries) by Neil Smith.
bestFor: UK-specific people search, records, and registries
trustNote: Long-running UK OSINT portal by practitioner Neil Smith.
notes: Specialized UK portal: electoral roll, company, land registry, and people-search pointers. High value for UK leads.

name: CSI Linux
description: Free investigation-focused Linux distribution/VM for OSINT and forensics.
bestFor: A pre-built OSINT/forensics workstation VM
trustNote: Established investigation-focused Linux distro.
notes: Bundles many OSINT/forensics tools in one VM; useful for isolating investigative work.

name: Kali Linux
description: Offensive-security Linux distribution with many recon/OSINT tools preinstalled.
bestFor: A versatile security/recon workstation
trustNote: Maintained by Offensive Security; industry-standard pentest distro.
notes: Includes OSINT tools (theHarvester, recon-ng, etc.) but is broadly pentest-oriented. Low direct relevance to person-finding.

name: Tsurugi Linux
description: DFIR and OSINT-oriented Linux distribution.
bestFor: A digital-forensics and OSINT workstation VM
trustNote: Established DFIR-focused Linux distribution.
notes: Strong for forensics with an OSINT module; mainly an investigator platform rather than a person-finding tool.

name: Trace Labs OSINT VM
description: Kali-based VM purpose-built for missing-persons OSINT (Trace Labs Search Party CTF).
bestFor: A ready-made toolkit specifically for missing-persons OSINT
trustNote: Trace Labs is a nonprofit that crowdsources OSINT to find missing persons.
notes: Purpose-built for missing-persons investigations; bundles the exact tools used in Trace Labs Search Party CTFs. Highly relevant. Trace Labs itself (tracelabs.org) is a key missing-persons OSINT organization to mine further.

name: Kasm Workspaces
description: Containerized disposable browsers and desktops for safe, attributable-free browsing.
bestFor: Disposable, isolated browser sessions to protect investigator identity
trustNote: Kasm is a well-known containerized streaming workspace platform.
notes: Free community edition self-hosted via Docker; hosted tiers are paid. Useful OPSEC layer when viewing subjects' content without leaking your real browser/IP.

name: Android Studio & SDK Tools
description: Official Android dev environment usable to run Android emulators for app-based OSINT.
bestFor: Running an Android emulator to investigate mobile apps safely
trustNote: Official Google developer tooling.
notes: Used to run mobile apps (e.g., social/dating apps) in an emulator for OSINT without using a personal device.

name: NCPTF (National Child Protection Task Force)
description: Nonprofit coordinating volunteers and law enforcement on missing/exploited persons cases.
bestFor: Missing-persons case coordination and trusted community
trustNote: Recognized nonprofit working missing-persons and child-protection cases.
notes: Organization rather than a tool; central to missing-persons OSINT community. Membership/vetting required to participate.
